title: Octets, Byte Streams, Fragmentation, Buffering, and Partial I/O · Contract Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective fragment-safe byte-stream reader and writer contract; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in fragment-safe byte-stream reader and writer contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Keep protocol data as octets until grammar permits text interpretation and distinguish ASCII tokens, opaque content, and invalid control bytes.
Interpret Read results using both byte count and error, including data with io.EOF, zero-byte reads, and temporary fragmentation.
Complete bounded output across short writes while retaining progress, error, and ownership evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Fragmentation Invariance
steps.instruction:
Order the Fragmentation Invariance evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Fragmentation Invariance.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Fragmentation Invariance assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Fragmentation Invariance, keep Fragmentation Invariance connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A debug protocol team handles case 4413 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve partial bytes while distinguishing fragmentation from incomplete input; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove the same bounded message result for every legal segmentation of an identical byte sequence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Fragmentation Invariance has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Order the regression proof · Debug Fragmentation Invariance, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Fragmentation Invariance precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Order the regression proof · Debug Fragmentation Invariance, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug protocol team handles case 4413 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve partial bytes while distinguishing fragmentation from incomplete input; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove the same bounded message result for every legal segmentation of an identical byte sequence.

For Order the regression proof · Debug Fragmentation Invariance, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A parser that passes one whole-buffer test is independent of network fragmentation.

options.text: Declare inputs, result and error contracts, ownership, and every goroutine stop condition for the debug Fragmentation Invariance case
Trace types, copies, aliases, deferred work, blocking points, and synchronization before editing for the debug Fragmentation Invariance case
Run the smallest isolated Go program and retain output or invariant evidence for the debug Fragmentation Invariance case
Change a boundary input or schedule, verify cleanup and races, then state toolchain limits for the debug Fragmentation Invariance case

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Fragmentation Invariance
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-fragmentation-invariance-1ylixc" that uses case 3424 to preserve partial bytes while distinguishing fragmentation from incomplete input. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.
